narrative: OMX30 faces headwinds from sharp European selloff overnight, with DAX and CAC both down >0.8% despite positive Asian close. US yields surging 63bp to 4.657% creates immediate DCF compression for Swedish Tech and Real Estate, while the strong ADX reading (42.5) with bearish directional bias signals conviction in the downturn rather than noise. RSI at 35.4 approaches oversold territory, raising mean reversion risk, but MACD remains below signal line and Bollinger positioning suggests support testing. Geopolitical risk (Houthis/Strait headlines) has failed to sustain oil strength (-8.15%), indicating market sees demand shock rather than supply panic.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens -0.3% to -0.6% tracking European red, with 3123 (yesterday close) as immediate support and 3085 (lower Bollinger band) the next floor. ADX 42.5 with bearish bias + MACD breakdown = trend-following selling likely to persist into cash open. Banks get modest help from yield steepness (+63bp US 10Y) but Real Estate (Balder, Castellum) faces refinancing headwinds; Tech/Telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon) downpressure from both NASDAQ sentiment (-0.57%) and DCF compression. Watch Industrials (Volvo, Atlas) for stabilization — still +6% vs 200-MA provides buyside anchor. US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET critical event; any weak print deepens risk-off, any beat could spark short-covering bounce into EOD.
drivers: European weakness (DAX -0.88%, CAC -1.09%) with strong ADX 42.5 bearish bias
US yields spike +63bp to 4.657% pressuring DCF valuations across Tech/Real Estate
VIX +7.63% to 17.91 signals risk-off mood despite Nikkei/Hang Seng gains
Oil crash -8.15% to 86.4 (despite headline geopolitical risk) suggests demand concerns

sectorImpacts.reason: NASDAQ -0.57% overnight + US 10Y yield +63bp creates dual DCF compression headwind. Valuation multiple reset risk in high-beta growth names.
US 10Y +63bp to 4.657% widens NIM expectations, offsetting broader risk-off sentiment. SEK weakness (EUR/SEK +0.14%) may marginally help funding spreads.
Yield spike to 4.657% significantly pressures refinancing costs and NAV multiples. High leverage exposure = asymmetric downside in repricing.

Oil -8.15% suggests demand softness trumping geopolitical risk narrative. Mixed bag: margin relief from lower input costs vs. cyclical growth concerns. USD/SEK flat (-0.05%) removes export FX tailwind.
Risk-off environment + VIX spike favors defensive rotation into healthcare names with stable cashflows, counteracting equity weakness.
Gold -1.24% and Copper flat +0.02% offer minimal support. Broader cyclical weakness dominates. SSAB exposed to weak industrial demand from DAX/CAC collapse.

watchList.reason: Industrials bellwether. Currently -0.88% EU correlation drag. Consolidating above 3030 support could signal stabilization if 3085 OMX30 support holds.
Banks most insulated from broader selloff given yield lift; +63bp US 10Y outweighs risk-off. Play for stability trade.
Double-hit from NASDAQ -0.57% + yield compression. RSI 35.4 may bounce, but ADX 42.5 bearish trend persistence favors shorts.
Real Estate most vulnerable to 4.657% yield level. Refinancing anxiety + multiple compression = asymmetric downside.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET is key event risk. Weak print (>250k) deepens risk-off, strong print (<230k) could spark short-covering bounce into EOD. No major Swedish macro releases.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (Houthis/Strait of Hormuz) could reverse oil narrative, triggering VIX spike above 20 and flash-crash lower in OMX30. RSI at 35.4 also signals mean-reversion risk if selling exhausts Friday session.
technicalSummary: MACD below signal line (bearish crossover confirmation)
ADX 42.5 with bearish DI bias = high-conviction downtrend, not noise
RSI 35.4 = near oversold, watch for bounces but ADX trend dominates
Bollinger %B=0.44 in lower half = room to test 3085 support
52W at 89% of range with -2.4% from high = distribution pattern forming

newsHeadlines.title: Oil jumps to $98 after Houthis attack two Saudi Arabian oil tankers
newsHeadlines.description: Yemeni rebels say they used missiles and drones to target the vessels Encelia and Layla

newsHeadlines.title: Military briefing: can the US reopen the Strait of Hormuz by force?
newsHeadlines.description: Renewed air campaign has yet to reassure shipowners the waterway can be made safe from Iranian attacks

newsHeadlines.title: US and Saudi Arabia agree landmark nuclear energy pact
newsHeadlines.description: Critics warn agreement lacks safeguards to prevent future uranium enrichment and reprocessing

newsHeadlines.title: Japan awakes
newsHeadlines.description: Why 1 per cent interest rates could shake everything up after a generation of deflation
